concerns.text: The spec narrows Phase 1 to alternating/non-alternating dichotomy only, deferring multi-class (torus, satellite, hyperbolic) exploration to Phase 2+. While documented as a 'deliberate scope boundary,' the original idea's research question encompasses broader prime knot classes. This is a fundamental scope reduction that redefines what the research can conclude in Phase 1. The spec claims 'research question remains consistent across all phases' but the actual testable question in Phase 1 excludes the full scope of the original research question.

Original idea specifies 'Split the dataset into training (80%) and hold-out test (20%) sets stratified by alternating/non-alternating classification.' Spec changes this to 'random stratified split by crossing number.' This is a methodological drift: the original stratification by alternating/non-alternating was essential to test whether 'the relationship differs systematically between alternating and non-alternating classes' (the core research question). Splitting by crossing number instead may not preserve the alternating/non-alternating distribution in validation samples, undermining the ability to answer the research question.

Spec adds computation of arc index, Seifert circle count, and bridge number—three additional invariants not mentioned in the original idea's methodology. The idea focuses on crossing number and braid index jointly predicting hyperbolic volume. While spec acknowledges this as 'exploratory extension,' it adds significant computational work (implementing Birman-Menasco, Seifert's algorithm, Schubert's decomposition) that was not part of the original scope. This is over-reach on supporting features.

Spec adds 'composite complexity score as a weighted combination of crossing number and braid index'—an exploratory construct not present in the original idea. The idea asks about 'joint prediction' via regression models, not about constructing composite scores. This adds implementation work (weight configuration, composite validation) that was not requested. Spec acknowledges this as 'exploratory construct not present in the original idea' but still includes it as a requirement.

Spec limits Phase 1 validation to crossing number ≤10 while idea specifies 'up to 13 crossings' for the prime knot census. Spec downloads ≤13 but only validates ≤10, marking 11-13 as 'exploratory/unvalidated.' This is a scope reduction that limits what can be concluded in Phase 1. While documented, this materially reduces the dataset size available for answering the research question in Phase 1 (prime knots at crossing 13 alone number ~9,988 per 2024 study cited in idea).
